---
trigger: glob
description: "Enforces strict TypeScript type safety and union narrowing."
globs: [src/**/*.ts, src/**/*.tsx]
---
<!-- Generated by /arch-review | Last updated: 2026-06-18 -->

# Type Safety Invariants

These rules enforce strict type safety, particularly around the core domain entity discriminated unions.

## Union Narrowing

### Mandatory Patterns
- **DO**: Use proper TypeScript type guards or discriminant checks (e.g., `if (entity.type === 'wall')`) to narrow types before accessing fields specific to a subclass.
- **DO**: Maintain the purity of the `Entity` discriminated union in `src/core/types.ts`.

### Prohibited Patterns
- ❌ Do not use `as any` or type assertions (e.g., `(entity as any).start`) to bypass the TypeScript compiler when accessing entity properties.
- ❌ Do not use the `any` type for variables, function parameters, or return types unless absolutely necessary (and if so, explain why in a comment).

## Known Violations (as of 2026-06-18)

- **[WARN-003]** `src/core/solver.ts`, `src/tools/select-tool.ts`, and other files: Pervasive type safety bypasses via `as any`.
